steph-thrive-notes:
💡 DANCER STRENGTH & PERFORMANCE PROGRAM
Source: @frankiebiondi.physio | Saved Mar 4, 2026

A 6-week small group strength program for competitive dancers, led by a registered physiotherapist (MScPT).

DETAILS:
• In-person, 8–10 dancers per group
• Thursdays starting July 2, 2026
• Location: Happiness is Dancing, Sudbury ON
• $600/dancer (tax included) + 8-week home program
• Age groups: 7 & under / 8–9 / 10–12 / 13+
• Limited spots — first come first served

WHY IT WORKS:
• Physio-led = trust + premium positioning
• Niche-specific (dancers only)
• Multi-week commitment = recurring revenue
• Age-tiered = wide audience from one program

THRIVE APPLICATION:
Could do something similar — e.g. Pilates for Dancers, Postpartum Strength, or Teen Athlete Performance. Partner with a local physio or RMT. Small group (6–10), multi-week, seasonal intensive. Great way to fill the barn outside regular class schedule.

steph-kids-stories-rush.title: Stinky Steve and the Missing Sock
Stinky Steve and the Sock Showdown
Cub the Grizzly
steph-kids-stories-rush.body:
Deep in the forest, under tall pine trees and big snowy mountains, lived a little grizzly bear named Cub.

Cub was strong. Cub was fast. And Cub loved to play rough. He liked to tumble, roll, and pounce in the grass.

"RAWR!" Cub would say as he jumped onto his friend Moose. Moose wobbled and laughed. "Cub! You're a rough player!"

Cub didn't mean to be rough. He was just excited.

One day Cub saw Bunny hopping through the meadow. "Let's play!" said Cub.

Cub pounced. Boop! Bunny rolled over in the soft grass.

"Cub!" Bunny giggled. "You're a little too strong!"

Cub's ears drooped. "I just want to play," he said quietly.

Old Mama Bear watched from under a big tree. She walked over and gave Cub a warm bear hug.

"Cub," she said gently, "you have a big grizzly body and a big grizzly heart. You just need to remember to use gentle paws with small friends."

Cub thought about that.

The next day he saw Bunny again. "Want to play?" asked Cub.

"Okay!" said Bunny.

Cub tiptoed closer. Instead of pouncing, he rolled softly beside Bunny. They tumbled in the grass. They laughed. They chased butterflies.

And when Moose came by, Cub gave him a big rough bear wrestle. Moose loved it. Because Moose was big enough.

That night Cub curled up in his cozy den. Mama Bear tucked him in.

"You played well today," she whispered.

Cub yawned a big grizzly yawn. "I'm a rough bear," he said sleepily.

"Yes," Mama Bear smiled. "But you're also a kind bear."

Cub closed his eyes. The forest grew quiet. The moon glowed over the mountains. And little Cub the grizzly dreamed of tumbling, playing, and using his gentle paws.

Goodnight, Cub. Goodnight, forest. 🌙🐻

steph-kids-stories-rush.title: Cub and the Big Forest Game
steph-kids-stories-rush.body:
In a wide green forest, under tall trees and bright stars, lived a little grizzly bear named Cub.

Cub loved to run, climb, and wrestle. He was very strong for a little bear. Sometimes when Cub played, he would tumble so hard that whoosh! everyone would roll over. But Cub was a very nice bear.

One morning Cub woke up early. The sun peeked through the trees. "Today is a perfect day to play!" Cub said.

He ran to the meadow where his friends were. Bunny was hopping. Squirrel was climbing. Moose was munching grass.

"Let's play the Big Forest Game!" said Cub.

"What's that?" asked Squirrel.

"It's a game where we run, hide, and chase!" said Cub excitedly.

"Okay!" said everyone.

"Ready… set… GO!"

Cub ran fast. Too fast! He zoomed past Bunny and made the grass whoosh. Bunny tumbled over.

"Oh!" said Cub, stopping quickly. "Sorry Bunny. I forgot my gentle paws."

Bunny stood up and shook the grass off. "That's okay," she said. "You're just a big, strong bear."

Cub thought for a moment. Then he had an idea.

"I'll be the tree guard," Cub said. "I'll stay by the big tree and tag anyone who runs by!"

The friends loved that idea. They ran around the meadow. Squirrel zipped past the tree. Tag! Cub gently tapped him. Moose trotted by. Cub gave him a playful nudge. And Bunny hopped right between Cub's paws.

Everyone laughed and laughed.

When the sun started to go down, the friends lay in the grass together.

"That was the best game," said Bunny.

"Yeah," said Squirrel.

Moose nodded. "Cub is the best tree guard."

Cub smiled a big grizzly smile. He liked playing rough. But he liked being a good friend even more.

That night Cub snuggled into his cozy den. Mama Bear tucked him in.

"Did you have a good day?" she asked.

Cub yawned. "The best," he said softly.

Outside, the forest grew quiet. The stars twinkled. And little Cub the grizzly fell asleep dreaming of running, playing, and all his forest friends.

Goodnight, Cub. 🌙🐻
